steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a punctuation question testing supplementary elements set off by paired dashes. The phrase "the cellular organs that generate energy from light" is a supplementary (non-essential) appositive defining "chloroplasts." A dash already follows that phrase, so the blank must supply an opening dash to form the required pair.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘chloroplasts’: no punctuation at all. Fails to open the supplementary element, leaving the appositive unseparated from the noun and the sentence grammatically incoherent. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘chloroplasts;’: uses a semicolon. A semicolon cannot pair with a dash to bracket a supplementary element; semicolons join independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘chloroplasts,’: uses a comma. A comma cannot pair with a dash to set off a supplementary element; the punctuation on both sides of the appositive must match. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘chloroplasts—’: uses an opening dash. Correctly pairs with the existing dash after "from light" to bracket the supplementary appositive, conforming to Standard English conventions. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
